questions.text:
There is an error in the following CSS rules. The error is in line:
```CSS
1. `em {font-weight: bold;`
2. `font-color: black; }`
3. `.special { font-style: italic; }`
4. `h1 {font-size: 12pt;}`
```
questions.explanation: In CSS, there is no property named `font-color`. To change the color of the text, the property is simply `color`. Line 2 should be `color: black; }`.

questions.text: The following URL `validator.w3.org/#validate-by-upload` is used for:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Rendering the output of the HTML Document

choices.label: B
choices.text: Correcting the errors in HTML document.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Checking the syntax of the CSS rules

choices.label: D
choices.text: Checking that an HTML5 document is syntactically correct

questions.explanation: The W3C Markup Validation Service (validator.w3.org) is a free service provided by W3C that helps check the validity of Web documents, such as HTML5, to ensure they are syntactically correct according to official standards.

questions.text:
Given the CSS rule below, which of the following elements would have the color red?

```CSS
.fav {color: red;}
```
questions.explanation: In CSS, selectors starting with a period (`.`) are class selectors. The selector `.fav` will match any HTML element that has an attribute `class="fav"`. Elements with `id="fav"` would require a hash selector (`#fav`).

questions.text: To link an external style sheet file named `myfavourite.css`, we type in the head:
questions.explanation: The standard HTML syntax for linking an external stylesheet is to use the `<link>` tag with the `rel="stylesheet"` attribute to define the relationship, and the `href` attribute to specify the file path. The `type="text/css"` attribute is also commonly included, though it is optional in HTML5.

questions.text: To insert an image named `x.jpg` of 100px width and 200px height in an HTML document, we type:
questions.explanation: In HTML, the `` tag uses the `src` attribute (source) to define the path to the image file. The `width` and `height` attributes are used to set dimensions. While the values in the options are swapped compared to the prompt's text, option B is the only syntactically correct choice because it uses the proper `src` attribute.

questions.text: Based on the provided browser preview image, which HTML/CSS code correctly styles the phrase "fourth year" in blue and shifted downwards?

choices.label: A
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University.

```

choices.label: B
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University.

```

choices.label: C
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University.

```

choices.label: D
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University.

```

questions.explanation: To shift an element downwards relative to its normal position using CSS relative positioning, you can use a negative value for the `bottom` property (e.g., `bottom: -2ex`). Since only the specific phrase "fourth year" is colored blue and shifted, it must be wrapped in a `<span>` tag. Option A would move the text upwards (`top: -2ex`), and Option D would move the entire paragraph.

questions.text:
Given the following CSS and HTML fragments, one of the following is true. html

Hello

Goodbye

choices.label: A
choices.text: Hello is blue, goodbye is red

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hello is blue, goodbye is green

choices.label: C
choices.text: Hello is red, goodbye is red

choices.label: D
choices.text: Hello is red, goodbye is green

questions.explanation:
In CSS, a direct element selector (like `p` or `em`) has higher specificity for that specific element than inherited styles from a parent container. For 'Hello', the `

` selector sets it to red, overriding the inherited blue from the `

`. For 'Goodbye', the `` selector sets it to green, overriding the inherited red from the `

`.

While option D has two buttons marked as `checked`, in standard HTML behavior for radio buttons with the same `name`, the last one would typically remain selected; however, this option is the most likely intended answer in the context of the exam's structure compared to checkboxes or un-styled/un-checked versions.

questions.text: The html Code which makes the link to the text "Hashemite university" is:
questions.explanation: In HTML, the `<a>` (anchor) tag is used to create hyperlinks, and the `href` attribute specifies the destination URL. Option B correctly identifies these components.

questions.text: Given the following CSS rule: `div p { background-color: #555555;}` which text "Corona Virus is gone" will apply this rule:
questions.explanation:
The CSS selector `div p` is a descendant selector. It targets all `

` elements that are descendants (children, grandchildren, etc.) of a `

` element. Only option C follows this structure.

questions.text:
What does the following code do?
`P {font-family: arial, helvetica;}`

choices.label: A
choices.text: It tells the browser to use both fonts every time.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It tells the browser to use Arial font always.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It tells the browser to use any font but not Arial or Helvetica.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It tells the browser to use Helvetica if Arial is not present.

questions.explanation: The `font-family` property lists font names as a fallback system. The browser will try to use the first font (Arial). If that font is not installed on the user's system, it will move to the next one (Helvetica).

questions.text: The `<title>` tag is used to

choices.label: A
choices.text: There is no tag in html called `<title>`

choices.label: B
choices.text: Give information about the table contents that help search engines finding a the web page

choices.label: C
choices.text: Display a title of the web page that appears in the first row of the page contents

choices.label: D
choices.text: Display a title of the web page in the browser toolbar

questions.explanation: The `<title>` element defines the title of the document. This title is displayed in the browser's title bar or the page's tab. It is a required element in all HTML documents.

questions.text: Based on the provided browser image showing a combined date and time selection field with the value `06/28/2013 03:19 AM`, which HTML code is correct?
questions.explanation: The `type="datetime-local"` input attribute is used in HTML5 to allow the user to select both a date and a time (without time zone information). Since the image shows both components, this is the correct input type.

questions.text: The HTML code which gives the following form is:
questions.explanation: While `type="name"` is not a standard HTML attribute value (the standard is `text`), browsers typically fall back to a text input field for unrecognized types. Option C is the only choice that includes the specific 'Name:' label text shown in the image alongside an input field that would appear as a text box.

questions.text: Based on the provided browser screenshot, which HTML code snippet correctly styles the link **Wadi Al-Rayan** to appear in red without an underline?

choices.label: A
choices.text: ```html Wadi Al-Rayan ```

choices.label: D
choices.text: ```html Wadi Al-Rayan ```

questions.explanation: In HTML/CSS, the property `text-decoration: none;` is used to remove the default underline from a link element (`<a>`). To change the font color to red, the `color: red;` property is applied. The visual output in the browser shows red text without an underline, making option D the correct choice.

questions.text: Observe the screenshot showing the text: "I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University". which of the following HTML structures would be required?

choices.label: A
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University

```

choices.label: B
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University

```

choices.label: C
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University

```

choices.label: D
choices.text:
```html

I am a fourth year student at Hashemite University

```

questions.explanation: To style a specific portion of text within a paragraph, an inline element like `<span>` is typically used. In this scenario, the paragraph is globally styled as blue, and the internal `<span>` tag overrides that color with red for just the words 'fourth year'.

questions.text:
What is the correct CSS syntax for making all the `

` elements bold?
questions.explanation: The `font-weight` CSS property is the standard way to set the thickness of text. The value `bold` specifically targets the bold weight of the typeface.

questions.text:
Given the following CSS rule:
`h1.x { color: font; font-size: 20pt; }` 
Which section applies this rule:
questions.explanation: The CSS selector `h1.x` is a combination of an element selector and a class selector. It targets any `<h1>` element that specifically has the attribute `class="x"`.

questions.text: Which of these HTML codes produce the table described below?

choices.label: B
choices.text:
```html ```
cascading Style Sheets
right left
shift

choices.label: C
choices.text:
```html
Cascading Style Sheets
left shift
right
```

questions.explanation: Option C correctly structures the table. The first row uses `colspan="2"` for the header. The second row defines 'left' and then 'shift' with `rowspan=2`. Because 'shift' occupies the second column for two rows, the third row only needs to define the 'right' cell for the first column.

questions.text: Which HTML code generates the following output?
questions.explanation: The registered trademark symbol (®) is represented by the HTML character entity `®`. `™` is used for the trademark symbol (™) and `©` for the copyright symbol (©).

questions.text: CSS stands for
questions.explanation: CSS stands for **Cascading Style Sheets**. It is a style sheet language used for describing the presentation of a document written in a markup language like HTML.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about meta elements is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: One way that search engines catalog pages is by reading the meta element's contents.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Meta elements are not visible to users.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Meta elements must be placed inside the head section of the HTML Document to be read by search engines.

questions.explanation: Meta elements provide metadata about the HTML document. They are located in the `<head>` section, are generally not visible to the user on the web page, and are used by browsers and search engines for processing and indexing.

The text 'Text' is inside a nested `<em>` element. This inner `<em>` is a descendant of the outer `<em>` which has the class `text`. The CSS rule `.text em` uses a class selector and a descendant combinator, giving it a higher specificity (0,1,1) than the other element-only selectors like `p em` (0,0,2) or `em` (0,0,1). Therefore, the color red is applied.

questions.text:
What does the following CSS rule mean:

```css
a:hover {color:green;}
```

choices.label: A
choices.text: When the mouse cursor is over the link, the whole document font color becomes green

choices.label: B
choices.text: When the mouse cursor is over the link, the font color comes back to blue

choices.label: C
choices.text: When the mouse cursor is over the link, the font color becomes green

choices.label: D
choices.text: When the mouse cursor is over the link, the whole paragraph font color becomes green

questions.explanation: The `:hover` pseudo-class selects an element when the user hovers over it with a mouse. In this rule, `a:hover` targets anchor (link) tags, and `{color:green;}` changes the text color of the hovered link to green.

questions.text: Which HTML code produces the following output?
questions.explanation: Option C is the only choice that correctly reflects the output text ('20' at the start and '100' at the end) and provides a meter value (5) that is approximately in the middle of the defined range (2 to 10), matching the visual state of the bar.

questions.text:
The HTML source code which gives the following output is:
**Nearly > 3/4 of our students love web prog.**

choices.label: A
choices.text:
```html

Nearly > ¾ of our students love web prog.

```

choices.label: B
choices.text:
```html

Nearly > 3/4 of our students love web prog.

```

choices.label: C
choices.text:
```html

Nearly > 3/4 of our students love web prog.

```

choices.label: D
choices.text:
```html

Nearly > ¾ of our students love web prog.

```

questions.explanation: In HTML, the character entity `>` is used to represent the greater-than symbol (`>`). Since the output displays literal characters '3/4' rather than the single-glyph fraction '¾', option B is the correct source code.

questions.text: Comments within HTML documents are enclosed within __________.
questions.explanation: In HTML, comments are defined by the `<!-- comment goes here -->` syntax. The other options are used for different programming languages (e.g., `/* */` and `//` are used in CSS, JavaScript, C++, etc.).

questions.text:
The HTML code which gives the following output is, where **me** has a link to the email address `ymkilani@hu.edu.jo`:

**For further information, please contact me.**

where the link is only to the word **me**.

choices.label: A
choices.text:
```html

For further information, please contact me.

```

choices.label: B
choices.text:
```html

For further information, please contact me.

```

choices.label: C
choices.text:
```html

For further information, please contact me.

```

choices.label: D
choices.text:
```html

a href = "mailto:ymkilani@hu.edu.jo"> For further information, please contact me.

```

questions.explanation: To create an email link in HTML, the `href` attribute must start with the `mailto:` protocol. Additionally, since the requirement states the link should only apply to the word "me", the anchor tag `` should be placed specifically around that word. Option C follows these requirements, even though the source text exhibits a missing closing `` tag.

choices.label: A
choices.text: ```html ```

choices.label: B
choices.text: ```html ```

choices.label: C
choices.text: ```html ```

choices.label: D
choices.text: ```html ```

questions.explanation:
The `z-index` property controls the vertical stacking order of elements; higher values appear on top of lower values. 
- Requirement: x is behind y (z-index of x < z-index of y), and y is behind z (z-index of y < z-index of z). 
- Looking at the classes: `.d3` (z-index: 4), `.d2` (z-index: 5), `.d1` (z-index: 10). 
- Thus, the stacking order is satisfied when x has class `.d3`, y has class `.d2`, and z has class `.d1`. Option D correctly assigns these classes.

Line 1 is missing curly braces. Line 2 uses incorrect syntax (square brackets instead of curly braces, space in class selector, and invalid property 'weight-color'). Line 3 uses an opening parenthesis instead of a curly brace and lacks a valid selector prefix. Line 5 uses an invalid property 'wd' and a closing parenthesis instead of a curly brace. Line 4 is the only valid rule.

questions.text: (CSS) A table takes 35% of the screen width, this can be written in HTML as:
questions.explanation: The standard and modern way to define an element's width in HTML is through the `style` attribute using the CSS `width` property. Option B is syntactically incorrect, and Option D incorrectly uses the `border` attribute for width.
